$comment: Per-competitor configuration. Read by collect-competitor-data.ts and generate-comparison.ts. Install instructions are 'as documented by the upstream README' — we do NOT optimise for our benchmark. Update with real install verification after VPS provisioning (see BLOCKED.md).

competitors.name: nox-mem

steps: cd memoria-nox && npm install && npm run build

competitors.notes: Reference implementation. The thing being benchmarked.

primary_source_claims.comment: We do not pre-fill with our own claims — these come from the harness.

competitors.name: agentmemory

install.daemon_notes: agentmemory requires a proprietary daemon (iii-engine) at runtime. Confirm whether the open-source agentmemory CLI can run without it before claiming 'self-host: yes'.

latency.notes: Subprocess invocation per query adds spawn overhead; documented in published numbers.

competitors.notes: README claims R@5 of 95.2% on LoCoMo. We do not preload that number — we re-measure.
blockers: Validate that iii-engine can be installed on our VPS without a paid license.
Confirm the published 95.2% R@5 corresponds to the same LoCoMo revision we use.

primary_source_claims.comment: From upstream README — re-measured by our harness.

competitors.name: memanto

latency.notes: Network round-trip to Moorcheh hosted endpoint included.

competitors.notes: Requires a Moorcheh account and API key. SaaS-only; cannot be run fully offline.
blockers: Acquire MOORCHEH_API_KEY with sufficient quota for full LoCoMo + LongMemEval run.
Document SaaS terms-of-service compatibility with our benchmark.

primary_source_claims.comment: From upstream marketing — re-measured by our harness.

competitors.name: mem0

latency.notes: Adapter scripts to be written; Python subprocess overhead documented.

competitors.notes: 53k+ stars. Vector + optional graph backend. Largest competitor by community.
blockers: Write Python adapter scripts for ingest/retrieve.
Pin embedding model — default is OpenAI, may need OPENAI_API_KEY budget.

primary_source_claims.comment: mem0 publishes its own benchmark on its docs; we re-measure with our harness.

competitors.name: letta

latency.notes: Local HTTP; recall-only mode (no agent loop) to keep retrieval comparison fair.

competitors.notes: 22k+ stars. Full agent runtime — we benchmark recall-only mode.
blockers: Confirm Letta API surface for recall-only ingest/retrieve (no agent loop).
Embedding key budget (Letta defaults to OpenAI).

primary_source_claims.comment: Letta's published numbers measure full agent loop; we measure retrieval only.

competitors.name: zep

locomo.retrieve: zep python SDK: zep.memory.search(session_id, text=query, limit=20)

longmemeval.retrieve: zep python SDK: zep.memory.search(session_id, text=query, limit=5)

latency.notes: We benchmark Zep Open Source (self-hosted) for the comparison; Zep Cloud noted separately if also measured.

data_format.notes: Zep has temporal KG — stronger on multi-hop temporal.

competitors.notes: Temporal KG memory; strong on multi-hop temporal queries.
blockers: Provision Postgres on VPS for Zep OSS.
Map Zep's structured-message format to LoCoMo's chunked transcript.

primary_source_claims.comment: Zep publishes its own LongMemEval numbers; we re-measure.

competitors.name: memorymd

steps: (no install — Claude Code's built-in MEMORY.md mechanism)

locomo.ingest: concatenate chunks into a single MEMORY.md per session
locomo.retrieve: no retrieval ranking — pass full MEMORY.md to composer

longmemeval.ingest: concatenate chunks into a single MEMORY.md per session
longmemeval.retrieve: no retrieval ranking — pass full MEMORY.md to composer

latency.notes: Filesystem-only baseline; latency is essentially file-read time.

competitors.notes: No-system baseline. Establishes the floor: what does an agent get with no memory layer at all? Useful for asking 'is the system better than doing nothing?'

primary_source_claims.comment: No published numbers. This is the floor.
